pullRequest.title: Add Copilot CLI as a first-class agent
pullRequest.body: ## Add Copilot CLI as a first-class agent Adds support for GitHub Copilot CLI as a built-in Harbor agent, runnable via `--agent copilot-cli`. **Changes:** - [copilot_cli.py](src/harbor/agents/installed/copilot_cli.py) - New `CopilotCli` agent extending `BaseInstalledAgent`. Installs Copilot CLI in Docker, runs in headless mode (`--output-format json`), parses JSONL output into ATIF trajectories, and handles auth errors gracefully. - [name.py](src/harbor/models/agent/name.py) - Added `COPILOT_CLI` to the `AgentName` enum. - [factory.py](src/harbor/agents/factory.py) - Registered `CopilotCli` in the agent factory. - [index.mdx](docs/content/docs/agents/index.mdx), [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) - Added `copilot-cli` to the agents list. **Usage:** ```bash harbor run --dataset terminal-bench@2.0 --agent copilot-cli --model copilot/claude-sonnet-4 ``` **NOTE:** * Copilot CLI uses its own model identifiers (e.g. `claude-sonnet-4`, `gpt-5.2`). Use the `copilot/` prefix in Harbor to route to the right model. * Authentication is via `GITHUB_TOKEN/GH_TOKEN/COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN`, or cached `gh auth`. Closes #218 ---

pullRequest.title: GPU support + example task that requires GPU
pullRequest.body:
This PR:
1. Added GPU as an environment config
2. Added an example task that requires GPU to run verifier
3. Verified modal sandbox could complete the example task: `uv run harbor run --agent oracle --path examples/tasks/ --task-name hello-cuda --env modal`

Discussion:
- how should we design abstraction around GPU? For now I just use a single number (like CPU cores) to define how many GPU cards you need, but I feel like that's a bad representation
  - update: added "gpu_types"

Closes #227

pullRequest.title: fix: add Alpine Linux support for claude-code agent
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #258

Changes:
- Add distro detection in claude-code install script
  - Alpine: uses apk to install curl, bash, nodejs, npm
  - Debian/Ubuntu: uses apt-get + nvm (unchanged)
- Add hello-alpine example task

Note: Alpine-based task containers must have bash installed in their Dockerfile for Harbor to work (Harbor uses bash for command execution).

pullRequest.title: fix: `--agent-import-path` ignored when `-a` not specified
pullRequest.body:
Fixes https://github.com/laude-institute/harbor/issues/261

## Summary

When using `--agent-import-path` to specify a custom agent without also passing `-a`, the custom agent was ignored and the default `oracle` agent was used instead.

## Changes

1. **Fix**: Clear `agent.name` when `import_path` is set via CLI so `import_path` takes precedence
2. **Improved display**: Show `import_path` in CLI output when agent name is None
3. **Testing**: Added integration tests with `MarkerAgent` example

### Before
```
$ harbor trials start -p task --agent-import-path my.module:MyAgent
Agent: oracle  ← Bug: custom agent ignored!
```

### After
```
$ harbor trials start -p task --agent-import-path my.module:MyAgent
Agent: my.module:MyAgent  ← Custom agent used!
```

## Testing

- 60 tests pass (57 unit + 3 integration)
- Red-green tested
- CLI end-to-end verified

pullRequest.title: Add get model limit utility and fix Terminus-2 error message
pullRequest.body: Fixes #344

pullRequest.title: Fix: Remove duplicate error_msg in OutputLengthExceededError handling
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes a bug where `error_msg` was being sent twice when an `OutputLengthExceededError` occurs in `terminus_2.py`.

Fixes: https://github.com/laude-institute/harbor/issues/371

## Problem

When `OutputLengthExceededError` is raised, the error message was being added to the chat history twice:

1. **Line 968**: Explicitly via `chat.messages.append({"role": "user", "content": error_msg})`
2. **Line 975**: Implicitly when `_query_llm(chat=chat, prompt=error_msg, ...)` is called, which internally calls `chat.chat(error_msg)` that automatically appends the prompt to messages

This resulted in the LLM receiving the same error message twice in the conversation history.

## Solution

Removed the redundant manual append on line 968. The `Chat.chat()` method already handles adding the prompt to the message history, so the explicit append was unnecessary and caused duplication.

## Changes

- **`src/harbor/agents/terminus_2/terminus_2.py`**: Removed the duplicate `chat.messages.append({"role": "user", "content": error_msg})` line

pullRequest.title: Fix: Remove untracked files before applying test patch in SWE-bench adapter
pullRequest.body: PR Description: Fixes #505 ## Problem Harbor's in-place evaluation leaves agent-created untracked files in the container. When the official test patch targets the same path as an agent-created file, `git apply` fails silently and the agent's file is used instead of the official tests. This leaves room for: - **False negatives**: Correct patches fail because agent's buggy test runs instead of official tests, which will not have the issue and succeed - **False positives**: Agents could game evaluation with fake passing tests ## Solution Add `git clean -fd` before applying the test patch to remove all untracked files, matching swebench.harness behavior. ```diff #First we reset all of the files which out test patch touches git checkout {base_commit} {" ".join(test_patch_files)} + #Remove untracked files to match SWE-bench harness behavior + git clean -fd #Then we apply the test patch given to us by swebench ``` Verification - Checked base SWE-bench docker images have no untracked files (git status shows "working tree clean") to be accidentally removed - Tested on django__django-16877 - fix correctly removes agent-created test files and runs official 4 tests instead of agent's 2 tests --- ---

pullRequest.title: Fix openrouter model name
pullRequest.body:
Fixing regression introduced by https://github.com/laude-institute/harbor/pull/339 : `openrouter/*`-prefixed model names resolved within LiteLLM stopped working. Previously you could just export `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, use `--model openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5` and everything worked.

Closes: https://github.com/laude-institute/harbor/issues/514

pullRequest.title: Fix NVM sourcing failure with strict mode
pullRequest.body: Fixes #538 NVM's `nvm.sh` returns exit code 3 internally during version checks, which breaks scripts using `set -euo pipefail`. Fixed by suppressing the false error and adding explicit verification: ```bash \. "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh" || true command -v nvm &>/dev/null || { echo "Error: NVM failed to load"; exit 1; } ``` Also added execution tests for all agent install scripts to catch similar issues. ---

pullRequest.title: Fix: dynamic context size for kimi-cli via LiteLLM model info
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Fixes #1185

- Replace hardcoded `max_context_size` (131072) with a dynamic lookup chain: `model_info` kwarg registration → LiteLLM database lookup → default fallback (131072)
- Follows the same pattern used by Terminus 2 for model info resolution
- Supports newer models like `kimi-k2.5` with 256k context windows without code changes

## Test plan

- [x] Unit test: `model_info` kwarg registers with LiteLLM and is used for context size
- [x] Unit test: falls back to LiteLLM database lookup when no `model_info` provided
- [x] Unit test: falls back to default 131072 when LiteLLM has no info

pullRequest.title: fix(modal): use find + concurrent downloads in download_dir()
pullRequest.body:
Fixes https://github.com/harbor-framework/harbor/issues/1216

## Summary

`ModalEnvironment.download_dir()` stalls when downloading directory trees with roughly 80+ files from a Modal sandbox. This causes `VerifierTimeoutError` and loses evaluation results including `reward.txt`.

The problem is that `download_dir()` uses `sandbox.ls.aio()` as a type probe on every child entry: call `ls(child_path)`, if it succeeds treat it as a directory and recurse, if it raises `NotADirectoryError` treat it as a file and download. For a tree with ~30 directories and ~90 files, this creates around 440 sequential RPCs. After 50-70 sequential `.aio()` calls, the Modal SDK's shared gRPC channel stalls indefinitely due to HTTP/2 flow-control starvation. The 60-second verifier timeout then fires and the results are lost.

The fix replaces the recursive ls-probe loop with a single `exec("find <dir> -type f")` call (1 RPC to enumerate all file paths) followed by `asyncio.gather()` with a `Semaphore(5)` for bounded-concurrent downloads. The method signature, docstring, and sandbox guard are unchanged.

This follows the same pattern already used in `RunloopEnvironment.download_dir()` ([runloop.py L434-455](src/harbor/environments/runloop.py#L434-L455)), which uses `find -type f` for tree enumeration.

## Before / After

| Metric | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Tree enumeration RPCs | ~150 (sequential ls probes) | 1 (single find exec) |
| Total RPCs | ~440 (all sequential) | ~291 (max ~20 in-flight) |
| Hits stall threshold? | Yes (around call 50-70) | No |
| Wall time (90 files) | >60s (timeout) | ~47s |

## Evidence

All runs from 2026-03-17 on Harbor 0.1.45 / Modal SDK 1.3.5 / Python 3.12:

- **Unpatched run**: `VerifierTimeoutError` after 60s, only 27 of 82 files downloaded, verifier phase took ~121s (60s + 60s retry) — [gist](https://gist.github.com/ashtonchew/35079504a9945819df56100c2d12af6e)
- **Patched run**: `reward=1.0`, all 82 files downloaded, verifier completed in ~33s — [gist](https://gist.github.com/ashtonchew/cf4639d54815a2b81f729a3f4f0fa0c7)
- **Standalone repro script**: isolates the stall outside of Harbor — [gist](https://gist.github.com/ashtonchew/26d0b9de70ea67cb07aa6fc8e1f4dcb4)

## Edge cases

- Empty directories return early
- `find` failure raises `RuntimeError` with stderr
- Individual download failures: all downloads run to completion, then the first error is re-raised
- Filenames with spaces work fine (one path per line from `find`)
- Public API is unchanged

## Related PRs

Complementary to #1202, which increases the file transfer chunk size from 8 KiB to 4 MiB in `upload_file()` and `download_file()`. That PR optimizes per-file throughput while this one reduces tree-level RPC count. No conflicts between the two.

## Test plan

Full methodology and before/after result logs: [testing gist](https://gist.github.com/ashtonchew/fda8fc8de8e0ed7d2e70ae075eb6b882)

- [x] All 683 existing unit tests pass
- [x] Ruff lint and format clean
- [x] ty type-check clean
- [x] 80+ file tree: `main` → VerifierTimeoutError (77/82 files), fix branch → reward=1.0 (82/82 files, ~47s)
- [x] Empty directory: passes on both `main` and fix branch (no regression)
- [x] `reward.txt` present and verifier returns expected score

pullRequest.title: fix(trial): strip trailing hyphens from truncated trial name
pullRequest.body: ## Summary - When a task name is truncated at 32 characters and ends with a hyphen, the generated trial name (e.g. `some-task-name-with-hyphens-__qxfbryt`) is rejected by Docker as an invalid tag reference format. - Fix: call `.rstrip('-')` after truncation in `generate_trial_name`. Fixes #1226 ## Test plan - [x] Verify task names that truncate at a hyphen boundary no longer produce invalid Docker tags - [x] Verify task names that don't end with hyphens are unaffected ---

pullRequest.title: fix(gke): break infinite retry loop when container is OOMKilled
pullRequest.body: ## Summary Fixes #1278. When a container is OOMKilled (or otherwise terminated) and the pod restart policy is `Never`, the verifier phase was entering an infinite retry loop — `_wait_for_container_exec_ready` retried 60 times per outer attempt, and the outer `@retry(stop=stop_after_attempt(5))` kept restarting it because no terminal condition was ever detected. - Adds `_check_pod_terminated()` which reads the pod status via the Kubernetes API - Called at the top of each iteration in `_wait_for_container_exec_ready` - Raises `RuntimeError` immediately if pod phase is `Failed`/`Succeeded`, or any container's current or last state is `Terminated` (catches OOMKill exit code 137 and all other terminal states) - Error propagates through `upload_dir` → verifier `AddTestsDirError` → trial exception recording, freeing the trial slot immediately ## Test plan - [ ] All existing unit tests pass (`uv run pytest tests/unit/`) - [ ] Manually verify on GKE with a task that triggers OOMKill — trial should fail fast with reward 0 instead of hanging ---

pullRequest.title: Add RolloutDetail.extra for provider-specific data (router indices)
pullRequest.body: ## Summary Adds a generic `extra` field to `RolloutDetail` that captures all non-`token_ids` fields from `provider_specific_fields`. This enables passing router expert selection indices (and any other provider-specific data) through the rollout pipeline for MoE model RL training. Closes #1288 **Motivation:** When training MoE models with RL, the inference engine (sglang) and training engine (Megatron) select different experts due to floating-point divergence, causing training collapse. The fix ([R3, arXiv:2510.11370](https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.11370)) requires capturing router indices during inference and replaying them during training. Upstream support is being added in [sgl-project/sglang#12075](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/issues/12075). **Approach:** Per @benediktstroebl's guidance, this uses `RolloutDetail.extra` as a generic `dict[str, list[Any]]` rather than a first-class field. The implementation is provider-agnostic: it passes through whatever `provider_specific_fields` contains (excluding `token_ids`, which is already handled separately). ## Changes - **`RolloutDetail`**: Add optional `extra: dict[str, list[Any]]` field - **`LLMResponse`**: Add optional `extra: dict[str, Any]` field for single-turn data - **`LiteLLM`**: Add `_extract_provider_extra()` to capture non-`token_ids` fields from `provider_specific_fields`, wire into `call()` - **`Chat`**: Accumulate extra data per-turn, pivot to per-field lists in `rollout_details` property (consistent with existing `completion_token_ids`/`logprobs` indexing) - **Terminus 2**: Handle `response.extra` in subagent rollout collection - **Tests**: 7 new unit tests covering extraction, accumulation, pivoting, and edge cases ## Data flow ``` sglang response -> choice.provider_specific_fields {"token_ids": ..., "routed_experts": ...} -> LiteLLM._extract_provider_extra() -> {"routed_experts": ...} -> LLMResponse.extra -> Chat._extra_list (accumulated per turn) -> RolloutDetail["extra"] {"routed_experts": [turn1_data, turn2_data, ...]} -> AgentContext.rollout_details -> trial result JSON ``` ## Usage Users opt-in to requesting router indices via `llm_call_kwargs`: ```bash harbor run ... --llm-call-kwargs '{"extra_body": {"return_routed_experts": true}}' ``` The exact `extra_body` key depends on the sglang API (being finalized in sgl-project/sglang#12075). No Harbor-side changes will be needed once upstream settles on a name -- the data flows through generically. ---

pullRequest.title: Fix duplicate legacy registry task expansion and harden registry sync
pullRequest.body: ## Summary Fixes #1315. This change addresses the duplicated task count seen when resolving datasets such as `swebenchpro` through the legacy Harbor registry path. - dedupe exact duplicate task rows returned by the legacy `get_dataset` RPC in the Harbor registry client while preserving first-seen order and logging a warning - detect and clean duplicate `dataset` rows in `scripts/sync_registry_to_supabase.py`, in addition to the existing duplicate `dataset_task` and `dataset_metric` cleanup - split dataset inserts from description updates in the sync script so registry writes do not depend on ambiguous dataset upsert behavior - add regression tests for live-registry RPC dedupe, duplicate dataset cleanup during sync, order preservation, and duplicate task-key detection in `registry.json` ## Why Issue #1315 reports that `harbor run -d swebenchpro` resolves 1462 tasks instead of 731. The checked-in `registry.json` is already clean, so the duplication is most likely coming from the live legacy Harbor registry backend or its sync state. This PR fixes the user-visible runtime symptom and also hardens the sync script against the most likely underlying source of duplication. ## Testing - `ruff check .` - `ruff format --check .` - `ty check --project /tmp/harbor-issue-1315 --python /Users/vuductai/Documents/Projects/harbor/.venv src/harbor/registry/client/harbor/harbor.py` - `PYTHONPATH=/tmp/harbor-issue-1315/src pytest tests/unit/test_harbor_registry_client.py tests/unit/test_sync_registry_to_supabase.py tests/unit/models/test_registry.py` - `UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache PYTHONPATH=/tmp/harbor-issue-1315/src pytest tests/unit/` ---
